boundary: This is the canonical overall inventory for capability status, dependency order, and assignment. It separates durable product capabilities from operator-owned external evidence, human decisions, and optional optimization; release-readiness authorities remain the delegated validators for their evidence gates. Local or fixture proof can complete a code capability, but it cannot promote hosted, release, or production evidence.
status_boundary: Capability status is a reviewed source-control judgment. Validation checks dependency shape, source anchors, release authority, and package commands without executing proofs or treating ignored target artifacts as fresh evidence.

items.title: Event store and replayable projections
items.summary: Append-only Postgres streams, optimistic concurrency, synchronous projections, and deterministic rebuild audits form the durable state boundary.
complete_when: Committed events rebuild every declared projection without drift.

items.title: Deterministic resolution engine and pack parity
items.summary: The closed rules IR and deterministic resolver cover the full source-derived engine inventory; this is local engine parity, not hosted product readiness.
complete_when: Every actionable in-scope engine inventory row is supported and validated, and every remaining unsupported row is explicitly classified out of scope.

items.title: Capability-gated command runtime
items.summary: Typed commands resolve authority at the boundary, append events transactionally, update projections, and return idempotent acknowledgements.
complete_when: Commands preserve capability, concurrency, idempotency, and event-log invariants through the Postgres pipeline.

items.title: Versioned wire and recoverable live delivery
items.summary: Versioned wire types, REST cold loads, WebSocket projection delivery, lag resync, and frontend hydration recovery are locally proven.
complete_when: A seeded client can cold-load, receive live updates, recover from lag, and retain capability-filtered state.

items.title: Host setup workbench
items.summary: The host setup role URL drives roster, role, policy, start-phase, stale-command rejection, and recovery through the real command path.
complete_when: A seeded host can configure and start a valid game from the setup role URL and recover from a stale mutation.

items.title: Seeded playable core loop
items.summary: Seeded host and player role URLs exercise posting, voting, actions, phase resolution, stale recovery, and completion in a real local game.
complete_when: The core live spine completes through browser role surfaces with its saved proof contract green.

items.title: Host and cohost moderation controls
items.summary: Host and delegated cohost controls cover deadlines, phases, prompts, publication, lifecycle changes, completion, conflict rejection, and reload recovery.
complete_when: Every shipped critical moderation control has a typed command, projection result, rejection boundary, and seeded role-surface proof.

items.title: Replacement continuity and authority transfer
items.summary: Replacement preserves slot history while transferring role URLs, sessions, actions, votes, and private-channel authority with stale outgoing denial.
complete_when: Incoming authority recovers across reconnect/reload and outgoing credentials cannot retain current slot capabilities.

items.title: Reveal-gated endgame and vote history
items.summary: Completed games reveal roles and resolved vote history only after completion and preserve them through stale recovery, resync, and reload.
complete_when: Normal and stale player surfaces converge on the same reveal-gated endgame summary and durable vote history.

items.title: Reference-backed private media baseline
items.summary: Generated tablet/small PNG references flow through SubmitPost, projection-backed serving, immutable headers, and member/non-member authorization; this does not prove upload, durable blob storage, metadata stripping, or transcoding.
complete_when: A command-backed private post serves referenced tablet/small bytes to a member and denies a non-member.

items.title: Canonical private media blob store
items.summary: Bounded local PNG/JPEG ingest applies EXIF orientation, serializes decoder-produced RGBA8 into a versioned canonical record, strips container metadata, derives BLAKE3 identity, and persists it through private fd-relative atomic storage with verified restart lookup; it does not normalize ICC profiles or expose upload, serving, or variants.
complete_when: Malformed and oversized inputs are rejected; metadata is stripped before BLAKE3 identity; equivalent images deduplicate; persistence is atomic and root-confined; restart lookup succeeds.

items.title: AVIF/WebP variant generation
items.summary: A pinned media recipe generates verified thumb, tablet, and full-bounded AVIF/WebP from canonical rasters, commits six private immutable members through a manifest installed last, and reproduces exact bytes across concurrency, restart, and explicit regeneration; this is local recipe proof, not a production codec-quality benchmark.
complete_when: The media core produces validated AVIF/WebP variants for every supported size and can regenerate them from canonical storage.

items.title: Authenticated bounded media upload
items.summary: Server startup now requires a pre-provisioned media root, and an active enabled account can submit bounded raw PNG/JPEG bytes to a prepare-before-persist endpoint that commits one canonical raster plus the fixed six-member variant set and returns only typed immutable handle metadata. Missing, non-account, expired, revoked, disabled, unsupported, mismatched, malformed, dimension-limited, body-limited, and variant-output-limited requests retain no canonical or variant bytes; this is local API/filesystem proof, not post serving, browser, hosted durability, or production upload readiness.
complete_when: Authorized uploads return a persistent handle while malformed, oversized, unsupported, and unauthorized requests fail without retained bytes.

items.title: Uploaded media through a private post
items.summary: Carry a real upload handle through SubmitPost, persistent projection serving, responsive rendering, and private-channel denial in a seeded browser flow.
complete_when: A seeded member uploads and posts media, receives the correct variant after reload, and a non-member receives no bytes.
remaining: Wire upload, post attachment, persistent serving, responsive UI, and seeded browser authorization proof.

recommended_slice.objective: Replace client-authored post-media URL maps with a server-issued content-id attachment contract: accept only an uploaded content id plus alt text at SubmitPost, verify the canonical handle before command acceptance, persist and project that immutable reference, derive private AVIF/WebP URLs server-side, and serve only manifest-backed variants after both account-session and projected channel-membership checks. Update the player composer to upload then post and render a responsive picture, and prove with the seeded live stack that a member receives real variant bytes after reload while a non-member receives no media bytes.
non_claims: No public or original-byte route, cross-post media library, gallery, profile-media, or moderation workflow.
No multipart/resumable upload, direct object-store upload, quotas/rate limits, orphan cleanup, or retention lifecycle.
No object-store/CDN integration, hosted durability, production performance, or production codec-quality claim.

items.title: Mafia private room
items.summary: A real private mafia day-chat room is pack-created, capability-filtered, command-backed, live, reloadable, and denied to non-members.
complete_when: Members can post and recover through the private role URL while non-members never receive room data.

items.title: Role private-message lifecycle
items.summary: Role-PM routes and frontend proof shapes exist, but a complete live create/post/recover/replacement lifecycle is not yet the durable product proof.
complete_when: Role PM creation, membership, posting, receipts, reload, replacement transfer, and stale outgoing denial pass through the real stack.
remaining: Add a seeded live role-PM lifecycle including replacement and stale-session boundaries.

items.title: Neighborhood, mason, dead, and spectator rooms
items.summary: The generic scoped-channel model exists, but the remaining room families and their encryption, lifecycle, replacement, and recovery behavior are not proven end to end.
complete_when: Every supported private room family is pack/config-created, encrypted at rest, capability-filtered, replacement-safe, and live/reload proven.
remaining: Implement and prove each additional private-room family across creation, membership, encryption, posting, and recovery.

items.title: Account login and account-bound invites
items.summary: Local accounts, opaque sessions, account-bound single-use invites, admin lifecycle controls, and the preserved role-surface adapter are locally proven.
complete_when: A real local account can log in, redeem only its invite, reach the intended role URL, and lose access when disabled or revoked.

items.title: Password storage and rotation security
items.summary: Argon2id storage, authenticated password rotation, session revocation, timing equalization, and bounded credential-attempt state are locally proven.
complete_when: Password verification and rotation preserve equalized failure work, revoke old sessions, and recover after bounded throttling.

items.title: Single-use account recovery
items.summary: Recovery credentials support issue, revoke, consume, expiry, replay rejection, password replacement, and session revocation in the local identity proof.
complete_when: Only one valid recovery credential can replace a password and all invalid, expired, revoked, or replayed credentials fail safely.

items.title: Complete session lifecycle
items.summary: Opaque issuance and revocation are implemented, but explicit logout plus periodic and privilege-change rotation remain incomplete.
complete_when: Login, logout, privilege change, password change, recovery, disablement, expiry, and periodic rotation each enforce the declared session transition.
remaining: Add explicit logout and declared periodic/privilege-change session rotation with focused proof.

items.title: Account registration
items.summary: There is no self-service registration product flow yet; existing accounts are created through administrative or proof setup paths.
complete_when: A bounded registration route creates an account, verifies policy, establishes the correct initial session state, and rejects duplicate/abusive attempts.
remaining: Design and implement the registration API, route, abuse boundary, and browser proof.

items.title: Invite and recovery delivery adapters
items.summary: Invite and recovery credentials exist, but durable delivery-provider adapters, retry state, bounce/failure handling, and operator visibility are not implemented.
complete_when: Invite and recovery delivery are provider-abstracted, retryable, auditable, redacted, and locally testable without claiming live provider traffic.
remaining: Implement delivery interfaces, queue/retry state, redacted operator audit, and local provider fakes.

items.title: Public game index
items.summary: The root board shell and per-game routes exist, but a durable public active/completed game index is not yet a complete product surface.
complete_when: The public board lists capability-safe active and completed games from a real projection with pagination and browser proof.
remaining: Build the game-index projection/query and replace the board shell with its real paginated surface.

items.title: Non-game discussion areas
items.summary: The product has game-scoped threads but no non-game discussion area, topic lifecycle, or moderation surface.
complete_when: Users can browse, create, post in, and moderate capability-scoped non-game discussions through real routes and projections.
remaining: Define and implement non-game discussion aggregates, projections, routes, moderation, and proof.

items.title: User profiles
items.summary: There is no public profile route or profile-editing lifecycle.
complete_when: A user can view and edit an authorization-safe profile, with durable projection state and browser proof.
remaining: Design profile events/projections, routes, privacy controls, editing, and media integration.

items.title: Portable completed-game export
items.summary: Completed games are durable event streams but cannot yet be exported as a self-contained, validated archive.
complete_when: A completed game exports its events, schema/version metadata, referenced projections/media manifest, checksum, and deterministic import/audit result.
remaining: Specify and implement the portable event-stream export format, CLI/API, checksum, and round-trip audit.

items.title: Real hosted identity evidence
items.summary: Local identity code and packet validators are not live hosted account, invite, recovery, abuse-control, session-secret, or retention evidence.
complete_when: A non-fixture redacted operator packet records passed real hosted identity lifecycle and role-surface evidence for every required family.
remaining: Deploy the identity lifecycle and attach real redacted retained/exportable evidence for every hosted family.
blocked_on: Real hosted identity target and operator evidence packet

items.title: Hosted API/frontend deployment evidence
items.summary: Local and hosted-like proof cannot establish an externally reachable deployment or external health checks.
complete_when: Externally reachable API and frontend targets pass the real-hosted preflight and raw-capture contract without fixture or synthetic inputs.
remaining: Provision the hosted target and capture non-synthetic external frontend/API health and flow evidence.
blocked_on: Externally reachable deployment and raw operator capture

items.title: Hosted demo fixtures and sanitized-data policy
items.summary: Local seed fixtures do not prove a hosted demo environment, sanitized data policy, or release-safe invite delivery.
complete_when: The hosted demo target is reproducibly seeded from sanitized policy-approved data and its invite delivery is release-safe.
remaining: Provision and audit hosted demo data, reset policy, and invite delivery.
blocked_on: Hosted demo environment and operator-approved sanitized-data policy

items.title: Production backup and recovery evidence
items.summary: The local backup/restore drill is baseline proof only; it does not establish production storage, PITR, key escrow, or secret rotation.
complete_when: A production-like backup, PITR restore, key escrow, and secret-rotation drill produces retained operator evidence.
remaining: Run and retain the production-like backup/PITR, escrow, and rotation drill.
blocked_on: Production-like storage, keys, secrets, and operator access

items.title: Real hosted concurrent-race evidence
items.summary: The promoted local race matrix does not prove multi-node hosted races, reload/reconnect convergence, or hosted stale-client behavior.
complete_when: The real hosted matrix executes concurrent commands across the deployed topology and records reload, reconnect, and stale-conflict convergence.
remaining: Execute and retain the non-fixture multi-node hosted race matrix.
blocked_on: Stable real hosted topology and external race capture

items.title: Real hosted observability and operations evidence
items.summary: Local structured logs and hosted-like signal bundles do not prove hosted logs, metrics, traces, paging/SLOs, or incident response.
complete_when: The deployed target emits retained logs, metrics, traces, alerts/SLO evidence, and an exercised incident-response receipt.
remaining: Capture real hosted telemetry, alerting/SLO, paging, and incident-response evidence.
blocked_on: Hosted telemetry stack and operator incident-response exercise

items.title: Human beta/release approval
items.summary: No coding agent or generated artifact can approve release ownership, rollback readiness, or support staffing.
complete_when: The release owner reviews product scope and all external evidence, verifies rollback/support staffing, and records explicit approval.
remaining: Obtain and record explicit human release-owner approval after all prerequisites close.
blocked_on: All required product capabilities and operator-owned release evidence

items.title: Record the strict vote-syntax decision
items.summary: The confirm-picker behavior is strict and shipped, but the architecture docs still describe strict tags versus freeform input as unresolved.
complete_when: The domain and roadmap docs record the shipped strict-target decision and remove the stale open call.
remaining: Update the governing design docs to match the shipped strict-target UI and command contract.

items.title: Ratify the fmarch codename
items.summary: The repository and packages use fmarch while the vision docs still call the product unnamed or TBD.
complete_when: The owner ratifies the name and the vision/index docs consistently use it.
remaining: Obtain the naming decision and update the governing docs.

items.title: Projection snapshots
items.summary: Snapshots are a discardable replay optimization and are excluded from required completion until measured replay cost demands them.
complete_when: A measured replay SLO breach is addressed by versioned discardable snapshots with deterministic fallback to full replay.
remaining: Define a replay benchmark and SLO before deciding whether snapshot implementation is warranted.
items.decision_trigger: Promote only when a representative long-game replay benchmark exceeds the declared latency or resource SLO.

orchestration_rules: The default coding selector may choose only dependency-satisfied incomplete code items; external evidence and human approval never become coding-agent work.
One integrator owns main, central registries, canonical artifacts, commits, and pushes; reviewers remain read-only while a slice is active.
A product-facing capability closes only with its listed acceptance evidence and explicit proof boundary; additional wrappers or commits are not progress by themselves.
Local, fixture, and hosted-like evidence may prepare operator handoffs but cannot complete an external-evidence item.
Optional work remains outside required completion until its decision trigger fires.
